mnemonics: LATCH before you CATCH: Latches are level-triggered (like a latch on a door stays open while the level is high), Flip-Flops are edge-triggered (they 'catch' the data on a clock edge).
keyPoints: Latches are transparent and level-sensitive; their output changes continuously while the enable signal is active.
Flip-Flops are edge-triggered; they only sample their input and change state at a specific clock edge (positive or negative).
The fundamental building block for sequential logic is the SR Latch, constructed from cross-coupled NOR or NAND gates.
commonMistakes: Assuming latches and flip-flops are synonymous and can be used interchangeably in a design.
Forgetting that metastability is a fundamental issue caused by setup or hold time violations, not just bad circuit design.
Incorrectly identifying the triggering mechanism (level vs. edge) of a given storage element symbol on a diagram.
